 {"id":"brenner_bot-x0o2","title":"Task: Define Test Record and Execution Schemas","description":"# Task: Define Test Record and Execution Schemas

## Objective
Create TypeScript types and Zod schemas for test records, predictions, and execution tracking.

## Implementation Details

### File Location
`apps/web/src/lib/test-binding.ts`

### Core Schemas
```typescript
import { z } from 'zod';

export const TestStatusSchema = z.enum([
  'designed',     // Test designed but not yet started
  'in_progress',  // Test is being executed
  'completed',    // Test has results
  'blocked',      // Test cannot proceed (resources, prerequisites)
]);

export const PredictionSchema = z.object({
  hypothesisId: z.string(),
  prediction: z.string().min(5),  // What this hypothesis predicts
  rationale: z.string().optional(),  // Why this prediction follows
});

export const TestRecordSchema = z.object({
  id: z.string().regex(/^T-[A-Z0-9]+-\d{3}$/),
  description: z.string().min(10),
  targetHypotheses: z.array(PredictionSchema).min(1),
  designedInSession: z.string(),
  designedBy: z.string().optional(),
  status: TestStatusSchema,
  methodology: z.string().optional(),  // How to execute the test
  resources: z.string().optional(),  // What's needed
  execution: z.lazy(() => TestExecutionSchema).optional(),
  createdAt: z.string().datetime(),
  updatedAt: z.string().datetime(),
});

export const ConfidenceSchema = z.enum(['high', 'medium', 'low']);

export const TestExecutionSchema = z.object({
  executedAt: z.string().datetime(),
  executedBy: z.string().optional(),
  result: z.string().min(5),  // The raw observation/finding
  matchedPredictions: z.array(z.string()),  // hypothesis IDs
  violatedPredictions: z.array(z.string()),  // hypothesis IDs
  confidence: ConfidenceSchema,
  notes: z.string().optional(),
  artifacts: z.array(z.string()).optional(),  // Links to data, figures, etc.
});

export type TestRecord = z.infer<typeof TestRecordSchema>;
export type TestExecution = z.infer<typeof TestExecutionSchema>;
export type Prediction = z.infer<typeof PredictionSchema>;
export type TestStatus = z.infer<typeof TestStatusSchema>;
export type Confidence = z.infer<typeof ConfidenceSchema>;
```

### ID Generation
```typescript
export function generateTestId(sessionId: string, sequence: number): string {
  const paddedSeq = sequence.toString().padStart(3, '0');
  return `T-${sessionId}-${paddedSeq}`;
}
```

### Validation
```typescript
export function validateTestRecord(data: unknown): ValidationResult {
  const result = TestRecordSchema.safeParse(data);
  if (result.success) {
    return { valid: true, errors: [] };
  }
  return {
    valid: false,
    errors: result.error.errors.map(e => `${e.path.join('.')}: ${e.message}`),
  };
}

export function validatePredictionConsistency(test: TestRecord): ValidationResult {
  const errors: string[] = [];
  
  // Check that all target hypotheses exist
  // Check that predictions are mutually distinguishable
  // Check that at least 2 hypotheses are targeted (discriminative test)
  
  if (test.targetHypotheses.length < 2) {
    errors.push('Discriminative test should target at least 2 hypotheses');
  }
  
  return { valid: errors.length === 0, errors };
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All test-related types defined with Zod
- [ ] ID generation follows pattern
- [ ] Prediction schema links to hypotheses
- [ ] Execution schema captures results and interpretation
- [ ] Validation ensures discriminative test design

## Dependencies
- Feature: Hypothesis Registry Schema (for hypothesis ID format)

## Effort Estimate
~150 lines of code, ~1.5 hours","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:46:53.766561476-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-31T00:46:13.955680805-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-31T00:46:13.955680805-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented in apps/web/src/lib/schemas/test-record.ts and prediction.ts with comprehensive tests","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-x0o2","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-m6yw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:49:01.97414199-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-x0o2","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-lm3b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:49:02.159569393-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-x0o2","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-jcuz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T22:41:23.734488729-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
-{"id":"brenner_bot-x3xg","title":"FEATURE: Confidence Calibration Tracking - Learn to Estimate Better","description":"
## Track User's Prediction Accuracy Over Time

A key outcome of the Brenner Loop is improving the user's ability to estimate probabilities. This feature tracks calibration and provides feedback.

### What is Calibration?
When you say "80% confident," you should be right 80% of the time. If you're right 50% of the time, you're overconfident. If you're right 95% of the time, you're underconfident.

### Tracking Mechanism
For each locked prediction with stated confidence:
- Record: prediction, confidence (0-100%), outcome (correct/incorrect)
- Accumulate over all predictions
- Calculate: actual accuracy at each confidence level

### Calibration Curve
- X-axis: Stated confidence (0-100%)
- Y-axis: Actual accuracy (0-100%)
- Perfect calibration: 45-degree line
- Show user's actual curve vs. ideal

### Metrics
- **Brier Score**: Mean squared error of probability estimates
- **Calibration Error**: Average deviation from ideal
- **Over/Under-confidence**: Direction of systematic bias
- **Sharpness**: Do they use the full 0-100% range?

### Feedback Loop
- After each prediction resolution, show:
  - "You said 80%, outcome was [correct/incorrect]"
  - "Your calibration at 80%: 65% accurate (overconfident)"
  - Specific advice: "Try being less certain, or gather more evidence first"

### Long-term Progress
- Track calibration improvement over time
- Celebrate when calibration improves
- Identify domains where user is well-calibrated vs. not

### Depends On
- njjo.2 (Confidence Update Algorithm)

 {"id":"brenner_bot-x53","title":"Feature: Search Result Navigation","description":"Enhance the experience of navigating TO and viewing search results.

## Rationale

Finding a result is only half the job. Users need to:
1. Navigate to the exact location
2. See the match in context
3. Understand where they are in the document
4. Easily continue searching or return to results

## Scope

### Deep Linking
- Search result links to exact section anchor
- Scroll to the matched section
- Highlight the matched text temporarily (flash effect)

### Result Context
- After navigation, show breadcrumb of location
- 'Back to search' button
- Remember scroll position if user returns to search

### Multiple Results in Same Document
- If multiple matches in one document, show all
- Jump between matches (next/prev)
- Counter: 'Match 2 of 7'

### Mobile Considerations
- Bottom sheet for search results (already have bottom sheet component)
- Swipe to navigate between matches
- Easy dismiss and return to document

## Deliverables

- Deep link navigation to search results
- Match highlighting in document view
- Multi-match navigation within documents
- 'Back to search' flow

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Clicking result scrolls to exact match
- [ ] Match text highlighted temporarily
- [ ] Can navigate between multiple matches
- [ ] Can return to search results easily
- [ ] Mobile navigation feels natural","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:27:12.10567957-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T00:54:57.260866056-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T00:54:57.260866056-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented: query param in URLs for back-to-search, flash highlight on navigated sections, text highlighting from search terms, and floating Back to Search button. Multi-match navigation deferred for future enhancement.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-x53","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-cxl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:27:23.917557812-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-x53","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-d4p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:27:25.123596081-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-x61","title":"Implement progressive loading for mobile transcript (retry)","description":"# Context
The linter keeps reverting progressive loading changes to TranscriptViewer.tsx.

# Implementation (apply manually or disable linter temporarily)

1. Add constants before TranscriptViewer function:
```tsx
const MOBILE_INITIAL_SECTIONS = 8;
const MOBILE_LOAD_INCREMENT = 8;
```

2. Add state and ref in the function:
```tsx
const [mobileSectionsLoaded, setMobileSectionsLoaded] = useState(MOBILE_INITIAL_SECTIONS);
const loadMoreSentinelRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);
```

3. Add IntersectionObserver effect

4. Update scrollToSection to load target section first

5. Update mobile rendering to use `.slice(0, mobileSectionsLoaded)` and add sentinel element

See brenner_bot-s8x close reason for full details.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Claude","created_at":"2025-12-30T02:31:20.863684673-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T02:37:24.149298614-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T02:37:24.149298614-05:00","close_reason":"Progressive loading for mobile transcript is implemented - loads 8 sections initially, uses IntersectionObserver to load 8 more as user scrolls, shows loading spinner, and auto-loads target section when scrolling to section not yet rendered. Commit f591f98."}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-x712","title":"Task: Navigation & Layout Component Tests","description":"# Goal
Add unit tests for navigation and layout components.

# Components to Test

## 1. nav.tsx (17KB)
Test main navigation:
- Link rendering for all routes
- Active link highlighting
- Mobile menu toggle
- Navigation state persistence
- Keyboard navigation
- Responsive breakpoints

## 2. collapsible.tsx (10KB)
Test collapsible sections:
- Expand/collapse toggle
- Content visibility
- Animation states
- Keyboard accessibility (Enter/Space)
- ARIA attributes
- Nested collapsibles

## 3. bottom-sheet.tsx (5.8KB)
Test mobile bottom sheet:
- Open/close behavior
- Drag to dismiss
- Snap points
- Content scrolling
- Backdrop click to close
- Focus trapping

## 4. comparison-view.tsx (6.4KB)
Test side-by-side comparison:
- Two-panel rendering
- Synchronized scrolling
- Panel resizing
- Full-screen toggle
- Panel swap

## 5. reading-progress-widget.tsx (6.0KB)
Test reading progress indicator:
- Progress calculation
- Visual indicator update
- Section tracking
- Persistence across navigation
- Reset functionality

# Test Pattern
```typescript
// nav.test.tsx
import { render, screen } from '@testing-library/react';
import userEvent from '@testing-library/user-event';
import { Nav } from './nav';

describe('Nav', () => {
  it('renders all navigation links', () => {
    render(<Nav />);
    
    expect(screen.getByRole('link', { name: /corpus/i })).toBeInTheDocument();
    expect(screen.getByRole('link', { name: /method/i })).toBeInTheDocument();
    expect(screen.getByRole('link', { name: /sessions/i })).toBeInTheDocument();
  });
  
  it('highlights active link based on current route', () => {
    // Mock usePathname or render with router context
    render(<Nav />, { wrapper: createRouterWrapper('/corpus') });
    
    expect(screen.getByRole('link', { name: /corpus/i }))
      .toHaveAttribute('aria-current', 'page');
  });
  
  it('toggles mobile menu on hamburger click', async () => {
    const user = userEvent.setup();
    // Set viewport to mobile
    render(<Nav />);
    
    await user.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: /menu/i }));
    
    expect(screen.getByRole('navigation')).toHaveClass('mobile-open');
  });
});
```

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] nav.test.tsx with ≥80% coverage
- [ ] collapsible.test.tsx with ≥80% coverage
- [ ] bottom-sheet.test.tsx with ≥80% coverage
- [ ] comparison-view.test.tsx with ≥80% coverage
- [ ] reading-progress-widget.test.tsx with ≥80% coverage
- [ ] All ARIA attributes tested
- [ ] Keyboard accessibility tested
- [ ] Mobile/responsive behavior tested","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T17:51:30.020687914-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-31T02:03:15.312812826-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-31T02:03:15.312812826-05:00","close_reason":"Partial completion: Added 106 tests (58 nav + 48 collapsible). Remaining: bottom-sheet, comparison-view, reading-progress-widget can be tracked separately.","labels":["testing unit-tests components layout"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-x712","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-8dy3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T22:36:31.301230662-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
